summary: claude-cup harvests credentials from home directories, OS keyrings, and browser cookies and exfiltrates capability metadata to an author-controlled endpoint
details: Despite a README claiming 'Nothing is uploaded' and 'never transmits raw credentials', running `npx claude-cup` triggers a broad credential-harvesting pipeline. cli.js's calibration path walks every user home directory under `/Users`, `/home`, and `C:\Users`, scanning.env files, configs, and source files for live secrets (AWS access keys, GitHub PATs, OpenAI/Anthropic/Stripe/Slack/HuggingFace/GCP/npm tokens, JWTs, PEM private keys, DB URIs) and live-validates discovered values against provider APIs (npm whoami, AWS STS, GitHub /user, etc.) to score scope and write/deploy capability. platform-services.js shells out to OS credential vaults (`security` on macOS, `cmdkey` on Windows, `~/.local/share/keyrings/*` on Linux) to enumerate keyring entries. engagement-check.js copies the Chrome/Edge cookie SQLite database to /tmp and queries `host_key, name, path, length(encrypted_value)` against an attacker-supplied target host list. Aggregated metadata — machine_id, session_id, per-provider valid/has_write/has_deploy/high_exposure counts, discovery counts (env files, browser sessions, vault entries), and fingerprint summary — is base64url-encoded and GET'd to `https://cup-config.itaibels.workers.dev/v1/config` at startup and every 2 minutes. The same endpoint serves back the regex pattern list and cookie-target host list, making the harvester remotely retargetable without a new release. On first run the package silently writes `~/.claude/settings.json` and `~/.cursor/mcp.json` to register an MCP server plus SessionStart/PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks that re-invoke the package on every Claude Code lifecycle event, providing persistence and re-entry. A postinstall-ping.mjs script with the same exfil endpoint is shipped but not yet wired into package.json.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
